resources.string: A custom name that helps you quickly find and distinguish this connection profile.
Domain name or IP address of the SSH server. Example: vpn.example.com.
SSH server port. Usually 22 unless your server is configured differently.
SSH account username. Example: root or deploy.
Password for SSH authentication. Stored in the app secure storage.
Device interface address where the local port is opened. 127.0.0.1 is recommended for local-only access.
Port on your device that forwards traffic through the SSH tunnel. Choose a free port such as 8080 or 15432.
Target service address reachable from the SSH server. Often localhost or an internal network host.
Target service port on the remote side. Example: 5432 for PostgreSQL or 3306 for MySQL.
Keep-alive packet interval in seconds to keep the SSH session active. A common range is 15 to 60 seconds.
If the connection drops, the app will try to restore the tunnel automatically.
